location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and one flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in program management or related fields
Compensation range $130,500–$179,500 consistent with mid-senior IC
Expected to build and scale programs from the ground up, manage BPO/vendor partners

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in program management or related fields (e.g., consulting, operations, project management or product operations), ideally within a technology, marketplace, or platform business.
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within a technology, marketplace, or platform business

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience managing cross-functional projects with measurable impact, including planning, execution, and stakeholder coordination.

Exposure to scaling operations through a combination of workflow automation and orchestration tooling (e.g., Zapier, Workato, Airflow, Google App Script, etc.), AI solutions, and effective use of BPO/vendor partnerships.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills; able to influence across technical and non-technical teams.
Strong analytical skills, with comfort pulling, interpreting, and acting on large datasets using Excel/Google Sheets, and BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Mode; SQL familiarity is a plus).
Ability to bring structure to ambiguity, manage competing priorities, and operate in a fast-paced environment.

scope.surface: Marketplace Operations (MOps) — scalable operational processes supporting brands and retailers on the Faire platform
scope.business_model: B2B wholesale marketplace (technology platform connecting independent retailers and brands)
core_responsibilities: Build and scale new programs from the ground up while optimizing existing operational processes
Leverage AI and workflow automation solutions to drive efficiency, accuracy, and long-term scalability
Define success metrics and monitoring systems in partnership with Strategy & Analytics
Develop Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), decision models, and escalation paths
Work with BPO and vendor partners to ensure high-quality delivery at scale
Partner cross-functionally with Strategy & Analytics, Strategy & Operations, Product, Engineering, and Data Science
Identify risks, surface insights, and drive day-to-day project coordination

green_flags: Clear team mandate and responsibilities described
Well-known, VC-backed company (Y Combinator, Sequoia, Founders Fund) with established marketplace product
Hybrid policy clearly defined (3 days/week in office with up to 4 weeks remote per year)
Explicit cross-functional partners named, reducing ambiguity about stakeholder landscape
red_flags: Role requires in-office presence 3 days/week in San Francisco; no indication of other city eligibility despite company having offices in Toronto, London, New York, and Kitchener-Waterloo
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not stated — unclear for non-US candidates
SQL listed only as a 'plus' but strong analytical requirement suggests SQL will likely be needed in practice

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from SF-specific salary range label, suggesting other locations may differ)
ambiguities: The JD salary line is labeled 'San Francisco, CA' only, but Faire has offices in Toronto, London, New York, and Kitchener-Waterloo. It is unclear whether this role could be based at another office location or whether remote outside SF is possible.
SQL is listed as 'a plus' in a sentence otherwise describing strong analytical requirements (must-have framing). Treated as soft/nice-to-have per JD language, but likely more important in practice.

The qualifications list uses unconditional bullet language for the workflow automation/BPO requirement ('Exposure to...') which could be interpreted as less than strictly required; treated as hard given unconditional placement in required qualifications section.
